mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending recession risk, triggering risk-off equity repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week as investors price in earnings contraction.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances alongside increasing housing starts indicate synchronized demand expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over a 5-year rolling window, or forward returns diverge from the predicted positive direction in more than half of the observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production sustaining above the historical trend level signals robust manufacturing output, driving upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns specifically during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO sustains values above 100 for three consecutive months, indicating the threshold no longer captures supply-side momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month inflation forces market pricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite inflation acceleration, or the signal fails to predict negative forward returns in a walk-forward test covering two distinct inflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong payroll growth and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market friction, increasing wage-push inflation expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern does not correlate with negative forward returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for concurrent changes in the federal funds rate.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly GDP growth expands the aggregate corporate earnings base, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through fundamental valuation support and reduced discount rate uncertainty.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC during periods of positive economic growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing construction exceeding 600k units annually alongside stable employment sustains consumer spending power, boosting cyclicals and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns specifically during low-volatility expansions.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero correlation with forward returns during high-volatility periods, or the predictive edge disappears when housing starts fall below the 550k threshold.
